topics.title: Input Cells
topics.summary: How to use input cells in MET Excel
content: In MET, you will have two different kinds of cells: Input Cells and Output Cells.
Input Cells are lightly colored cells that require user input.
You can either type into the cell or select from a dropdown menu.
Input Cells provide the information necessary to formulate Output Cells.
To clear an Input Cell, select 'No' from dropdown or press Backspace.
tips: Look for lightly colored cells - these are your inputs
Dropdowns make selection faster and reduce errors
Backspace clears any input cell quickly

topics.title: Output Cells
topics.summary: Understanding output cells and estimate lines
content: Output Cells are the estimate lines that appear based on your inputs.
Output Cells dynamically change based upon your Input Cells.
These are the lines you will export to CCC or Mitchell.
tips: Output cells update automatically as you fill in inputs
Review output cells before exporting to ensure accuracy

topics.title: Reading Notes (Red Arrows)
topics.summary: How to read Microsoft Excel notes in MET
content: Microsoft Excel Notes have a Red Arrow in the top right corner of the cell.
To read the note, hover your mouse over the red arrow.
Notes contain important information throughout MET.
These notes provide context, P-page references, and helpful tips.
tips: Always check for red arrows - they contain valuable info
Notes often reference P-pages or DEG inquiries
Hover don't click to see the note content

topics.title: Navigating Tabs
content: Located at the bottom of Excel will be several tabs.
Tabs categorize operations per Estimate area (Body, Mechanical, Refinish, etc.).
You can navigate left to right or click on the 3-line button for a tab list.
When writing an estimate or repair plan, it is recommended to go through ALL tabs.
tips: Don't skip tabs - each one has unique operations
Go systematically from left to right for best coverage
Use the 3-line menu for quick tab jumping

topics.title: Gathering Vehicle Info (Car in Front of You)
topics.summary: Essential information to collect when the vehicle is in front of you
content: When the vehicle is in front of you, gather ALL essential information FIRST:
1. VIN NUMBER - Locate on dash (driver's side) or door jamb
- Decode the VIN to get exact year, make, model, trim, engine
- Verify it matches the estimate/assignment
2. MILEAGE - Record current odometer reading
3. PAINT CODE - Find on driver's door jamb sticker or under hood
- Record both the color code and variant/toner codes if present
- Take a photo of the paint code label
4. PRODUCTION DATE - On the same label as paint code
- Important for part compatibility and OEM procedures
5. PRE-SCAN - Run diagnostic scan BEFORE any disassembly
- Document ALL DTCs (Diagnostic Trouble Codes)
- Screenshot or print the scan results
6. PHOTOS - Take comprehensive photos:
- 4 corners of vehicle
- All damage areas (close-up and wide shots)
- VIN plate, paint code, mileage
- Any pre-existing damage
7. OPTIONS/EQUIPMENT - Note ADAS features:
- Blind spot monitors
- Forward collision warning
- Lane departure / Lane keep assist
- Adaptive cruise control
- 360 cameras / parking sensors
8. TIRE INFO - Record tire sizes and condition
- Note if mismatched or worn
9. KEY TYPE - Standard, proximity/push-button, or dead key fob?
tips: ALWAYS verify VIN matches the assignment before starting
Take MORE photos than you think you need
Pre-scan is REQUIRED for all vehicles - document it
Paint code photos save time when ordering materials
Note all ADAS features - they require calibrations
Check for aftermarket parts or modifications

topics.title: Using the Clip It Button
topics.summary: How to copy operations from MET Excel
content: Step 1: Locate the 'Clip It' button in MET Excel and LEFT CLICK it.
Step 2: Press Ctrl + C to copy the selected operations.
The operations are now on your clipboard ready for export.
tips: Left click only - don't double click
Ctrl+C copies the formatted operations
The MET Desktop tool will detect the clipboard automatically

topics.title: Exporting to CCC ONE
topics.summary: How to paste operations into CCC ONE using MET Desktop Tool
content: Step 1: Copy operations from MET Excel (Clip It + Ctrl+C).
Step 2: Open CCC ONE and click in column A where you want to paste.
Step 3: Click the 'CCC Desktop' button in MET Desktop Tool.
The tool will automatically type each operation into CCC ONE.
Input is blocked during paste so you can't accidentally interfere.
tips: Make sure CCC ONE is open before clicking export
Click in column A where you want operations to start
The tool handles everything - just wait for it to finish
If needed, Ctrl+Z in CCC to undo

topics.title: Part Codes Setup in CCC
topics.summary: Setting up part codes for faster estimating
content: To work efficiently, add placeholder Part Codes in CCC.
When you select Part Codes during estimating, you have quick placeholders to different areas.
Recommended base part codes to add:
- Placeholder for Body operations
- Placeholder for Mechanical operations
- Placeholder for Refinish operations
- Placeholder for Miscellaneous operations
You can add more as needed - these 4 are a great baseline.
tips: Set up part codes once, use them forever
Part codes let you quickly jump to estimate sections
Customize part codes for your shop's workflow

topics.title: Using MET Desktop Tool
topics.summary: How this tool works with MET Excel
content: MET Desktop Tool monitors your clipboard automatically.
When you copy from MET Excel, operations appear in the Export tab.
Click the 'CCC Desktop' button to export directly to CCC ONE.
The tool uses smart automation to paste safely - input is blocked during paste.
The tool stays on top so it's always accessible while you work.
tips: Keep MET Desktop Tool running while estimating
The tool stays always-on-top for easy access
Use the Export tab to see what's on clipboard
Check the Reference tab for P-pages and DEG info
Use the Guide tab (this tab!) for help anytime

topics.title: Installation
topics.summary: How to install MET Desktop Tool
content: Download from the provided link or OneDrive location.
Save to an accessible location in your file explorer.
Windows may show a security warning - click 'More info' then 'Run anyway'.
The download is a secure Windows Application.
tips: Save to a location you can easily find (Desktop or Documents)
Security warnings are normal for downloaded apps

topics.title: Activation
topics.summary: How to activate MET Desktop Tool
content: Double-click the application to launch.
If prompted, input your licensed email address.
You will receive either 'Valid' or 'Invalid' response.
Valid = Activated and ready to use.
Invalid = Not authorized or incorrect email/spelling error.
tips: Check email spelling carefully
Use the exact email that was licensed
Contact support if you get Invalid with correct email

topics.title: Troubleshooting
content: If clipboard appears empty: Make sure you copied from MET Excel (Clip It + Ctrl+C).
If not authorized: Verify you're using the correct licensed email.
If Invalid response: Check for spelling errors in email.
If paste isn't working: Make sure CCC ONE is open and you clicked in column A.
Contact Mcstudestimating@gmail.com for support.
tips: Most issues are email spelling related
Make sure MET Excel has the Clip It range selected before copying
Restart the tool if clipboard isn't updating

export-tab.title: Export Tab Help
export-tab.description: This tab shows your clipboard contents and lets you export to CCC or Mitchell.
steps: 1. Copy operations from MET Excel (Clip It + Ctrl+C)
2. Operations appear in the preview below
3. Click in CCC ONE column A where you want to paste
4. Click 'CCC Desktop' button to export
5. Wait for paste to complete - input is blocked during paste
tips: Keep CCC ONE open before clicking export
Click in column A first, then click export button
Check operation count matches what you expected

import-tab.title: Import Tab — Estimate Analyzer
import-tab.description: Upload or paste an existing estimate so McStud can read it, find what's missing, and learn patterns for next time.
steps: 1. Upload a PDF (drag-drop or browse) OR paste estimate text from CCC (Ctrl+A → Ctrl+C → Ctrl+V) and hit Parse
2. Parsed lines appear in green — every part, operation, labor hour, and dollar amount the parser found
3. Click 'Build Operation Lines' to get suggested missing ops for THIS estimate (nothing is saved)
4. Click 'Learn from This' to teach McStud the patterns (e.g. Front Bumper + Replace → typical ops) for FUTURE estimates
5. Check the purple Suggestions panel for items the knowledge base flagged as missing (adhesion promoter, blend, flex additive, etc.)
6. Use 'Add High Priority' to grab critical suggestions, or select individual items
tips: Works with CCC ONE, Mitchell, and Audatex estimate formats
Drag multiple PDFs at once for batch learning
Shorthand is auto-normalized: LT→Left, Frt→Front, Bpr→Bumper, R&I→Remove & Install
Quality score tells you if an estimate looks like an outlier — you can still learn from it
Learned data shows up in the Learned tab where you can search and review patterns
Great for supplement review — paste the original and see what was missed

chat-tab.title: Chat Tab Help
steps: 1. Type your question in the chat box
2. Get answers about procedures, P-pages, operations
3. Copy useful info to your estimate notes
tips: Ask about specific operations
Request P-page references
Get help with ADAS calibrations

stats-tab.title: Stats Tab Help
stats-tab.description: View your export statistics and performance metrics.
steps: 1. Select time period (daily, weekly, monthly)
2. View export counts and totals
3. Track your productivity over time
tips: Stats help identify patterns
Compare performance across periods
Track labor hours exported

reference-tab.title: Reference Tab Help
reference-tab.description: Quick access to definitions, P-pages, DEG inquiries, and procedures.
steps: 1. Select category (Definitions, P-Pages, DEG, Procedures)
2. Search or browse topics
3. Click to expand details
4. Copy to clipboard for use in estimates
tips: Use search for quick lookups
P-pages support your billing
DEG inquiries are industry rulings

settings-tab.title: Settings Tab Help
steps: 1. Adjust paste speed for your computer
2. Check for updates
3. View version information
tips: Slower paste speed is more reliable on older computers
Keep auto-update enabled for latest features

guide-tab.title: Guide Tab Help
guide-tab.description: Learn how to use MET Excel and MET Desktop Tool.
steps: 1. Use search to find specific topics
2. Expand sections to read detailed guides
3. Check Quick Answers for common questions
tips: Search for keywords like 'paste' or 'copy'
Each section covers a different aspect of MET

shop-docs-tab.title: Shop Docs Help
shop-docs-tab.description: Access and print shop checklists, forms, and documents.
steps: 1. Select a checklist from the dropdown
2. Optionally enter an RO number
3. Click 'Copy' to copy as text or 'Print' to open printable version
4. Print opens in your browser - use Ctrl+P to print
tips: RO number will appear on printed checklist
Required items are marked with red asterisk
Use checklists to ensure nothing is missed

chat-subtab.title: Chat Help
chat-subtab.description: Ask questions about estimating, operations, and MET features. The chatbot understands collision repair terminology.
steps: 1. Type your question in the input box at the bottom
2. Use quick-reply buttons for common questions
3. Operations mentioned in answers can be copied to clipboard
4. Pin important messages for quick reference
tips: Ask about specific operations like 'R&I bumper cover'
Use categories bar to filter question types
Chat history is saved between sessions

learned-subtab.title: Learned Patterns Help
learned-subtab.description: View patterns the tool has learned from uploaded estimates. Shows which operations commonly appear together.
steps: 1. Browse learned operation patterns
2. See frequency and confidence scores
3. Patterns improve as you upload more estimates
tips: Upload more estimates to improve pattern accuracy
High-confidence patterns are most reliable
Patterns are used to suggest missing operations

assistant-subtab.title: Estimator Assistant Help
assistant-subtab.description: Get guided help building estimates with step-by-step assistance.
steps: 1. Select the type of damage or repair
2. Follow the guided prompts
3. Review suggested operations
4. Export operations to your estimate
tips: The assistant walks you through common repair scenarios
Suggestions are based on real estimate data
You can modify suggestions before exporting

build-estimate-subtab.title: Build Estimate Help
build-estimate-subtab.description: Search for operations and build an estimate line by line. The related operations panel shows co-occurring items that typically appear together.
steps: 1. Type an operation name in the search box
2. Click a suggestion to add it to your estimate
3. Check the 'Related Operations' panel for commonly paired items
4. Review your estimate in the right panel
5. Click 'Export' to send operations to the Export tab
tips: Related operations are based on real estimate patterns
The percentage shows how often items appear together
Use short keywords like 'bumper' or 'blend' for faster search

ghost-compare-subtab.title: Ghost Estimate Comparison Help
ghost-compare-subtab.description: Compare your estimate against AI-generated 'ghost' estimates to find missing operations.
steps: 1. Upload or paste your current estimate
2. The tool generates a comparison ghost estimate
3. Review highlighted differences
4. Accept suggested operations to add them to your estimate
tips: Green items are in your estimate, red items may be missing
Click 'Accept' on individual operations to add them
Ghost estimates are suggestions - always verify with your judgment

screen-ocr-subtab.title: Screen OCR Monitor
screen-ocr-subtab.description: Watches your CCC ONE or Mitchell window in real time, reads the estimate via OCR, and coaches you on what's missing — like having a senior estimator looking over your shoulder.
steps: 1. Open your estimate in CCC ONE or Mitchell so the line items are visible
2. Click 'Start' — the monitor captures your screen every few seconds and reads the text
3. 'Estimate Analysis' shows the parts and operations it detected, grouped by part
4. 'Live Coaching' scores your estimate (A+ to F) and flags missing operations by priority (Critical, High, Medium, Low)
5. 'Potential Recovery' shows the dollar amount you may be leaving on the table
6. 'Similar Estimates' finds past jobs like this one so you can compare what you wrote before
7. Use 'Feed to Chat' to send the captured data to the AI assistant for questions
8. Use 'Copy' to grab the coaching suggestions as text
tips: Navigate to the estimate LINES page in CCC — the monitor reads line items, not the summary page
The coaching score updates live as you add operations — watch it improve in real time
Critical/High missing items (pre-scan, post-scan, OEM research) are the most commonly underbilled
Add custom SOP items via the gear icon to flag shop-specific operations
Works best when the full operations list is visible — scroll through CCC slowly if needed
The monitor only READS your screen — it never types or modifies anything in CCC

quickAnswers.what info do i need from the car: VIN, mileage, paint code, production date, pre-scan, photos, ADAS features, tire info, and key type.
quickAnswers.what to do when car arrives: 1) Verify VIN 2) Record mileage 3) Find paint code 4) Pre-scan 5) Take photos 6) Note ADAS features
quickAnswers.where is the paint code: Driver's door jamb sticker or under the hood on a label. Take a photo of it.
quickAnswers.how do i copy: Use the 'Clip It' button in MET Excel, then press Ctrl+C.
quickAnswers.how do i paste to ccc: Click in column A in CCC ONE, then click the 'CCC Desktop' button in MET Tool.
quickAnswers.what is an input cell: A lightly colored cell where you type or select from dropdown.
quickAnswers.what is an output cell: The estimate lines generated from your inputs.
quickAnswers.what are red arrows: Excel notes - hover over them to read important information.
quickAnswers.how do i navigate tabs: Click tabs at the bottom of Excel or use the 3-line menu.
quickAnswers.why is paste not working: Make sure CCC ONE is open and you clicked in column A before clicking export.
quickAnswers.how do i clear a cell: Select 'No' from dropdown or press Backspace.
quickAnswers.how do i export: Copy from MET Excel, click in CCC column A, then click 'CCC Desktop' button.
